title: Machine-Verified SQG Shear-Vorticity Identity + Conditional Theorem 3 Roadmap (Lean 4)
description: Lean 4 + mathlib formalization of Fourier-space identities for the Surface Quasi-Geostrophic equation, working towards a machine-checked regularity proof. Covers Theorems 1 and 2 of paper D14 (shear-vorticity identity Ŝ_nt - ω̂/2 = |k|·sin²(α-β)·θ̂ and selection-rule bound) plus supporting Riesz/Sobolev infrastructure on the 2-torus. §10 adds a conditional Theorem 3 (SQG regularity) roadmap with named axiomatic hypotheses pinning down exactly which analytic facts the regularity argument borrows. FracSobolevCalculus is discharged unconditionally. BKM's axiomatic scope is reduced first to s > 1 (§10.6 interpolation) and then to the integer-order range s ∈ (1, 2] only (§10.8 S2 bootstrap, BKMCriterionS2) — avoiding fractional calculus entirely. MaterialMaxPrinciple alone handles s ∈ [0, 1]; the combined Theorem 3 on s ∈ [0, 2] rests on integer-order axioms only. v0.4.1 added Fourier convolution scaffolding, mode-Lipschitz keystone, SQG Bochner wiring. v0.4.2 closed the Duhamel keystone end-to-end: concrete sqgNonlinearFlux realization of (u·∇θ)̂, ℓ²-control helpers, Parseval split-at-zero-mode and full Fourier-tsum L² conservation, and the internalized promotion SqgEvolutionAxioms_strong.of_sqgDuhamelIdentity_via_MMP. v0.4.3 adds §10.20–§10.22: concrete mollifier family (sqgConcreteMollifier = product of smoothTransitions), RHS bump-to-indicator limit via DCT (sqgConcreteMollifier_rhs_tendsto), and the full LHS collar FTC — monotonicity + derivative sign & vanishing + FTC mass identities + integral split + collar squeeze — delivering SqgFourierContinuous.toCollarLhsCondition that closes Phase 2.3.b of the bump-to-indicator bridge. §10.23 then ships the first non-zero concrete discharge: IsSqgWeakSolution.const_zero_u (Duhamel witness for constant-in-time θ with zero velocity), MaterialMaxPrinciple.of_const and BKMCriterionS2.of_const (both trivial via le_refl for constant solutions), and the capstone sqg_regularity_const that certifies uniform Ḣˢ bounds on [0, 2] for any Ḣ¹-summable constant-in-time scalar field — unconditionally. v0.4.4 adds the first multi-mode stationary SQG witness: §10.29 odd-symmetry helpers (latticeNorm_neg, rieszSymbol_neg, sqgVelocitySymbol_neg, derivSymbol_neg); §10.30 antipodal-pair construction singleMode m₀ a₁ + singleMode (-m₀) a₂ with its Riesz-transform velocity, four divergence-free identities unified under sqgVelocitySymbol_mul_derivSymbol_sum_zero_antipodal_pair, the per-ℓ cancellation antipodal_inner_sum_zero, and the main theorem sqgNonlinearFlux_antipodalMode_eq_zero (flux vanishes at every target mode); §10.31 bundles into SqgEvolutionAxioms_strong.antipodalMode_const — the first multi-mode discharge of the strong axioms via of_IsSqgWeakSolution_via_MMP. All content machine-verified with zero sorry across 11452 lines. Mathematical theorems by Bryan Sanchez (independent researcher); Lean formalization by Bryan Sanchez with AI assistance.
